evals.prompt: Create a Temporal workflow for processing document conversions. It should download a file, convert it to PDF, and upload the result. Each step should have retry logic.
evals.expected_output: Activities file, workflow file, exports, worker registration, and backend service to start it
expectations: Activities defined in packages/temporal/activities/ with @activity.defn decorators
Workflow defined in packages/temporal/workflows/ with @workflow.defn decorator
RetryPolicy configured with maximum_attempts for transient failures
Activities and workflow exported in packages/temporal/__init__.py
Worker registration shown with workflows=[] and activities=[] lists
Backend service checks temporal.is_enabled before starting workflow
Workflow ID is deterministic (includes document/file identifier)

evals.prompt: Build a durable workflow for multi-step user onboarding: create account, send welcome email, provision resources, notify admin.
evals.expected_output: Multi-activity workflow with proper error handling and retry
expectations: Each step is a separate @activity.defn function (not all in one)
Uses dataclasses for input/output types, not raw dicts
Workflow imports use workflow.unsafe.imports_passed_through() context manager
Activities have individual timeout configurations appropriate to their work
Workflow orchestrates activities sequentially (each depends on previous)
